title: dendrolobium rostratum
intro: Dendrolobium rostratum, also known as the Horse Fiddle or the Spurred Pea, is a shrub native to Australia and New Guinea. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The leaves and pods of Dendrolobium rostratum have been used to create herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which may provide health benefits such as relieving congestion, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing. However, its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional, as some sources suggest potential toxicity and allergic reactions.
